title: cryptogramma crispa subsp. acrostichoides
intro: Cryptogramma crispa subsp. acrostichoides, also known as the Rocky Mountain fern or alpine rock fern, is a type of fern native to the mountainous regions of western North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cryptogramma crispa subsp. acrostichoides include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
